moduleTitle: Visual Appearance of Discontinuities, Evaluation, and Reporting

lessons.title: Manufacturing and Inherent Discontinuities
objectives: Recognize the visual appearance of primary manufacturing discontinuities such as those in castings
Recognize secondary manufacturing discontinuities such as those in forgings
Describe inherent discontinuities and their origins in the material

lessons.title: Service-Induced Discontinuities
objectives: Recognize discontinuities caused by mechanical loads such as fatigue cracking
Recognize discontinuities caused by thermal loads, corrosion, and abrasive wear
Relate service conditions to the expected discontinuity type and location
cp105Topics: 7.3.2 Caused by thermal loads (e.g., thermal shock)

lessons.title: Evaluation Schemes, Criteria, and Techniques
objectives: Apply the general evaluation scheme of ASTM E1316 terminology
Use verbal descriptions, comparison standards or catalogs, size-based criteria, and mixed criteria to evaluate indications
Perform evaluation by visual-tactile recognition, grading by comparison with a standard, and measurement
cp105Topics: 8.3.2 Grading by comparison with a standard

lessons.title: Reporting, Documentation, and Test Completion
objectives: Prepare technique reports, data reports, and image reports including sketches and photographs
Confirm completion of testing using a checklist
Maintain documentation that supports traceability and review
cp105Topics: 8.4.3 Image reports (sketches, hard-copy photo, or digital photo)
8.5 Completion of testing confirmed with a checklist
